title: Kiper's favorites from the 2026 NFL draft class: 15 players who will outperform their draft slot

title: Sources: Giants trade Lawrence for Bengals pick
content: The Giants are trading defensive tackle Dexter Lawrence to the Bengals in a deal for the No. 10 pick in next week's NFL draft, sources told ESPN.

title: LeBron plays 'triple threat' to spring Game 1 upset
content: With Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves sidelined, LeBron James ignited the Lakers' offense with "a little bit of everything," posting 19 points, 13 assists, 8 rebounds and 2 steals in a Game 1 upset of the Rockets.

title: Barkley: Jordan rift easing, golf game planned
content: Charles Barkley says he and Michael Jordan are talking again after nearly 15 years and that the two plan to play golf together in the offseason.

title: Buoyed by Brunson, Knicks seal 'hard-fought win'
content: Jalen Brunson scored 22 of his game-high 28 points in the first half as the Knicks posted a 113-102 victory over the Hawks in Game 1 of their Eastern Conference first-round series.

title: Murray's FT dominance irks Wolves, lifts Nuggets
content: Jamal Murray had a tremendous night at the free throw line, making all 16 of his attempts to carry the Nuggets past the Timberwolves in Game 1, during which Nikola Jokic and Aaron Gordon had a quiet first half.

title: Mullins, projected 1st-round pick, to stay at UConn
content: UConn's Braylon Mullins, who was ranked No. 17 on ESPN's NBA draft big board, will look to boost his stock with a second season in Storrs.

title: Mets in free fall after losing 10th straight game
content: The Mets lost their 10th straight game Saturday, the team's longest skid since 2004. Only three teams have made the postseason after a losing streak that long.

title: Skydiver crashes into Hokies' scoreboard, rescued
content: A skydiver was rescued and in stable condition after crashing into the Lane Stadium scoreboard before Virginia Tech's spring football game.

content: Imposition of conditionalities in the 2023 Act was the ‘real betrayal’, and their reinforcement through the amendment Bill is a direct insult to all women, says the National Coalition for Women’s Reservation

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Voters miffed at apathy towards industrial potential of Aravakurichi constituency
content: Aravakurichi is one of the drought-prone belts in Karur district. Drinking water remains a major issue.

title: State Food Commission unearths large-scale irregularities in ration distribution in Edamalakkudy
content: During visits to ration shops in Societykudy and Vellavarakkudy, the commission found that no registers were maintained for the arrival or distribution of goods

title: Centre’s potato procurement price is just half of input cost, allege farmers’ bodies
content: While the Union Agriculture Ministry on Saturday approved procurement at ₹6.5 per kg, a farmer leader says input cost itself is ₹12 per kg. A farmer can make at least a small profit only if the price is at least ₹15 per kg, he says.

title: Terms of engagement: a video series
content: Terms of Engagement is a series that will host experts who think and work on questions related to India’s federalism, identity and representation, in order to facilitate this conversation.

title: Vizhinjam port sees surge in vessel traffic amid Hormuz Strait shutdown: Tharoor
content: Positioning the port in Thiruvananthapuram alongside established global hubs such as Colombo and Singapore, Congress MP says that it is emerging as a major player in global maritime trade by setting a new standard

title: Kozhikode DIET throws open its doors for science enthusiasts
content: A science lab, named after noted scientist E.C.G. Sudarshan, has been set up in five halls at the institute

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Srivaikuntam constituency residents flag infrastructure woes, livelihood issues
content: Srivaikuntam town which has witnessed a rise in pilgrimage tourism in recent years, continues to await basic infrastructure facilities such as well maintained roads, clean drinking water and proper sewage system.

title: Study assesses risks from hanging glaciers in Central Himalaya
content: The study identifies 219 such unstable glaciers, sensitive to warming temperatures and shifting weather patterns, in the Alaknanda basin of Uttarakhand

title: No evidence of drunk driving against Malayalam actor Maniyanpilla Raju in accident case: police report
content: Accident took place on February 5 when the actor’s car collided with a motorcycle, leaving two youths injured. The incident had sparked controversy as the actor did not stop his vehicle immediately after the crash

title: Loan app operators suspected behind youth’s disappearance in Vadakara
content: According to a complaint filed by the family, Vishnu Prakash, who went missing on April 13, had been under severe stress following repeated harassment and intimidation by some agents associated with a digital lending app

content: Preliminary reports indicate that a lorry carrying garlic from Haryana was driven in a rash and negligent manner causing the casualties

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Cumbum constituency farmers and the unkept promises on Mullaperiyar Dam
content: Farmers here say that their grievances have never been addressed by the politicians, who make tall promises only during the poll time and forget conveniently for the next five years.

content: The blueprint envisions the State as a key driver in India’s industrial decarbonisation journey, while significantly enhancing MSME competitiveness

content: Fisheries department officials and marine enforcement squad were part of the inspection carried out on Sunday

content: SNDP Yogam leader says such discussions should take place only after election results are announced. He adds that competition for power in the Congress is not new

title: Rajnath Singh to visit Germany to boost defence ties
content: Discussions to focus on enhancing defence industrial collaboration, expanding military-to-military engagements, and exploring cooperation in emerging domains such as cybersecurity, artificial intelligence and drone technologies

title: Chamarajanagar Deputy Commissioner says no blazers, ties, or heavy uniforms for students amid heatwave
content: As the Chamarajanagar district recorded a maximum temperature of 38.8 degrees, all educational institutions were instructed to make adequate provisions for clean drinking water in all rooms

title: Replicate Annamayya district’s water conservation model across Andhra Pradesh, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: Announcing the appointment of the district’s Collector as nodal officer for Jaladhara programme, the Chief Minister stresses the need for repairing canals and tanks to prevent water-logging and filling water bodies during monsoon season

title: Teachers’ forum seeks key reforms, filling of vacancies in schools
content: Leaders urge the government to entrust works under the “Mana Badi – Mana Bhavishyathu” programme to contractors, say that quality teaching will be possible only if they are strictly confined to teaching duties and relieved from non-teaching responsibilities

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Manamadurai constituency voters seek tangible changes
content: While the constituency encompassed three blocks – Thirupuvanam, Ilayangudi and Manamadurai – none of them had witnessed any industrial or education growth in the past five years, the public felt.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Sivaganga constituency, an administrative capital eclipsed by Karaikudi’s political clout
content: During the 1983 reorganisation of Ramanathapuram district by the M.G. Ramachandran administration, Sivaganga was intentionally selected as the administrative capital instead of Karaikudi, primarily due to its significant cultural heritage and regional importance.

title: Sivankutty slams Thiruvananthapuram Corporation over alleged failures in waste management
content: Minister alleges that the BJP’s promise of a blueprint for the city’s development within 40 days of coming to power was an eyewash, as there is no sign of such a plan even months after taking charge

title: VMC mulls bringing water from Andra reservoir to meet water needs of city
content: The Fort City needs around 47 million litres per day. However, the availability is said to be below 35 MLD during April and May months

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Nanguneri constituency, a dry agrarian region with no dearth of caste issues
content: Since this dry agrarian region gets water from Manimuthar dam whenever the district’s largest reservoir overflows, crop cultivation, mostly paddy and banana, is based on the release of water from this dam.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Paramakudi constituency struggles with infrastructure gaps, untapped potential
content: Despite the strategic advantage of having one of the major river systems in the State, the local economy—defined by its reliance on agriculture and weaving—continues to face limitations in accessing the source’s full potential.

title: Delimitation decoded: What the Centre tried to do, why oppn shot it down & what happens next
content: A government bill linking women's reservation to a landmark seat expansion in the parliament and state assemblies was defeated on the floor of the Lok Sabha. The opposition called it a victory against delimitation "by the backdoor." Here is what the bill sought to do, why it collapsed, and what the constitutional fight over representation will look like next.

title: 'Will knock out every power plant’: Trump warns Iran as US delegation heads to Pak
content: A US delegation is heading to Islamabad for fresh talks with Iran after previous negotiations faltered. President Trump issued a stern warning, vowing severe action if Iran rejects the "fair and reasonable" deal offered. Tensions remain high following disagreements over Iran's nuclear program and sanctions, with the Strait of Hormuz becoming a focal point of the dispute

title: Amazon internal document reportedly points to an 'AI mess'; says: AI is making our…
content: Amazon's rapid AI adoption is creating a significant internal infrastructure problem, leading to a surge of duplicate tools and orphaned data. The company's decentralized structure exacerbates this issue, as autonomous teams build independently without checking for existing solutions. This results in a growing, unmanageable mess of redundant systems and persistent derived data, even after original sources are removed.

title: Deepika expecting 2nd baby with Ranveer, makes announcement via social media
content: Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh are expecting their second child, with Deepika sharing a cute social media post hinting at her baby bump. The couple, married in 2018 in Italy, already welcomed daughter Dua in 2024, and fans are thrilled as their family grows once again.

title: OpenAI exec Srinivas Narayanan leaves to spend time with ageing parents in India
content: Srinivas Narayanan, OpenAI's CTO for B2B applications, is departing the company next week after nearly three years. He plans to visit his parents in India before considering future professional endeavors. Narayanan, who previously served as VP of Engineering, shared his departure on LinkedIn, calling his tenure at the AI firm 'three incredible years'.

title: IPL: KKR snap losing streak as Rinku Singh holds nerve in RR last-over thriller
content: Kolkata Knight Riders ended their six-match winless streak in IPL 2026, securing a four-wicket victory against Rajasthan Royals at the Eden Gardens on Sunday. Rinku Singh's unbeaten 53 guided KKR to the target with two balls to spare, a much-needed win after repeated collapses. The win lifts them off the bottom of the table.

title: 'Do you eat onions?' PM Modi takes 'Jhalmuri break' during Bengal poll campaign - watch
content: Amidst a high-stakes political campaign in West Bengal, Prime Minister Narendra Modi took a refreshing pause for a jhalmuri snack in Jhargram. He interacted with the vendor, inquiring about the price and confirming his liking for onions with a witty remark. This candid moment unfolded as the Prime Minister intensified his campaign efforts across the state.

title: Maharashtra couple fight over half-cooked mutton, woman later kills herself
content: A Yavatmal homemaker died after consuming poison. Chetana Jadhav reportedly took the extreme step following an argument with her husband over undercooked mutton. The dispute occurred in front of a guest. Chetana felt humiliated and consumed poison later. She was rushed to the hospital but declared dead. Police have registered a case against her husband.

title: Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu to engineers on how to survive in AI era
content: Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu advises software engineers to prioritize deep domain expertise over just programming skills to succeed in the AI era. While AI accelerates prototypes, he emphasizes that delivering security, reliability, and compliance is what customers truly value. Vembu urges teams to focus on enhancing customer experience with AI, not just programmer productivity, by eliminating unnecessary complexity.

title: No fuel shortage: Govt assures 100% domestic LPG, PNG, CNG supply amid energy crunch
content: Amidst geopolitical tensions, the government assures uninterrupted fuel availability, with no disruption in LPG supply and normal deliveries. Citizens are urged to avoid panic buying as efforts are underway to ensure petrol, diesel, and LPG availability. Indian shipping continues to navigate the Middle East safely, with authorities closely monitoring the situation and ensuring the safety of all seafarers.

title: Jofra Archer scripts history vs KKR, becomes only second bowler to...
content: Opening the bowling for Rajasthan Royals, Archer bowled KKR opener Tim Seifert for a golden duck with the first delivery of KKR’s innings. With this, Archer now has five wickets on the first ball of an IPL innings, matching Shami’s record.

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce plan wedding move amid security concerns
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are gearing up to get married after being together for close to three years now. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have been rumored to be tying the knot on 3rd July in New York City, details have been kept private. Amid all this, it seems the high profile couple is focused on giving back to society.

title: Tamil Nadu blast: 18 workers killed in Virudhunagar firecracker factory
content: A devastating blast at a firecracker factory in Tamil Nadu's Virudhunagar on Sunday claimed 18 lives and injured six. Chief Minister MK Stalin expressed deep sorrow, extended condolences to the bereaved families, and dispatched ministers to oversee rescue efforts and provide immediate assistance.

title: 'Not in lifetime': Lalit Modi vs Pietersen explodes over The Hundred's IPL comparison
content: The exchange follows Modi’s earlier remarks where he said The Hundred would not survive beyond three years. Pietersen disagreed with that view and said the competition would continue, possibly in a T20 format.

title: Jaipur nikah, Kashmir trail: How LeT's 'Khargosh' managed to flee India
content: A top Lashkar-e-Taiba operative, Umer Harris alias “Khargosh,” has reportedly fled India to Saudi Arabia using a forged passport. The operative, originally from Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, married under an assumed identity in Jaipur, which aided his passport application. This revelation highlights significant loopholes in India's verification systems.

title: Dalal Street this week: US-Iran tensions, crude swings and Q4 earnings to drive markets
content: Dalal Street eyes US-Iran conflict, crude oil, and corporate earnings for direction. HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank reported Q4 results, with the former warning of West Asia conflict risks. Analysts anticipate continued focus on geopolitical developments and their impact on global markets and capital flows.

title: Bohiragoto vs Bhadralok: Bengal’s identity battle heats up as parties 'fish' for votes
content: As the campaign sharpens, the familiar charge of “bohiragoto” has returned to the centre of the political stage, with the All India Trinamool Congress (TMC) reviving a narrative that helped it decisively blunt the rise of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) five years ago. The immediate trigger this time, as seen in the reaction to Leander Paes joining the BJP, shows how quickly the labels are deployed as "outsider, parachuted, not one of us".

title: Slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei yet to be buried. Are Iranian officials too 'afraid' to do so?
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's burial remains uncertain over a month after his death, with officials citing security concerns and an "unprecedented turnout" expectation.  A joint US-Israeli airstrike on February 28 escalated into broader conflict, delaying the planned state funeral.  Concerns include potential Israeli strikes and nationalist counter-rallies, with Mashhad being considered as a burial site.

title: India-US trade deal: Three-day talks to begin from April 20; what to expect
content: India and the United States will resume trade talks next week in Washington. A delegation of Indian officials will meet their US counterparts. Discussions will focus on the first phase of a proposed bilateral trade agreement. Recent changes in US tariff policy necessitate a review of the earlier finalized trade structure.

title: 'Mr Prime Minister, speak from ... ': Mamata's dare to Modi over women's quota bill address
content: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee hit back at PM Modi's criticism regarding the Women's Reservation Bill, accusing him of misleading the nation. She defended her party, TMC, stating its consistent support for women's representation. Banerjee clarified her opposition is to the delimitation exercise, which she believes is a political ploy, and questioned the bill's delayed implementation and linkage to delimitation.

title: Market recap: Eight of top 10 firms add Rs 1.87 lakh cr in mcap; Airtel biggest gainer
content: Indian markets concluded the week on a positive note, with the combined market value of the top ten companies surging by over Rs 1.87 lakh crore. Bharti Airtel emerged as the leading gainer, adding Rs 58,831 crore to its valuation. This uptrend was supported by easing geopolitical tensions and a positive risk sentiment.

title: ‘You can’t breach a contract you never received’: Muzarabani’s camp slams PSL ban
content: Zimbabwean pacer Blessing Muzarabani faces a two-year PSL ban for withdrawing from a deal, but his agency vehemently denies any wrongdoing. They assert no formal contract was signed, making the ban unjustified.  While the league cites a breach of commitment, the agency calls the penalty excessive and urges its withdrawal, highlighting an administrative error amidst Muzarabani's IPL success.

title: Two Indians shot dead outside Italy gurdwara during Baisakhi gathering
content: Two Indian men were fatally shot outside a gurdwara in Covo, Italy, on Friday night after a Vaisakhi festival gathering. Authorities are investigating the incident as a potential "coldly planned execution," with initial findings suggesting the attack was not spontaneous. Shell casings were recovered, and a witness described the shooter as an Indian who also frequented the place of worship.

title: ‘Turn around immediately’: Moment Iranian Navy blocked Indian vessel in Strait of Hormuz - Watch
content: Two Indian-flagged tankers, Bhagya Lakshmi and Sanmar Herald, were denied passage and reportedly fired upon by Iranian gunboats in the Strait of Hormuz. Crew members of Bhagya Lakshmi were told to turn back, while Sanmar Herald reported being fired upon despite prior clearance. India has raised concerns with Iran over the safety of merchant shipping.

title: ‘A little tacky’: Trump reviews his own McDonald’s moment
content: A staged Oval Office McDonald's delivery to celebrate a tax policy has drawn criticism, with President Trump himself calling the event "tacky." The DoorDash driver, Sharon Simmons, who previously advocated for the policy, was highlighted for alleged savings. DoorDash clarified it was a planned event, not a genuine delivery, defending Simmons' participation.

title: Embattled UK PM Starmer To Face Grilling From MPs Over Mandelson Scandal
content: Embattled UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer will face lawmakers in parliament on Monday as he tries to quell anger over an unrelenting scandal involving long-time Jeffrey Epstein associate Peter Mandelson.

title: Indian Tanker Crosses Hormuz As 2 Vessels Forced To Turn Back After Firing
content: Crude oil tanker Desh Garima, with 31 Indian seafarers onboard, crossed the strategic waterway on April 18 and is expected to reach Mumbai on April 22.

title: Thousands Greet Amit Shah At Big Chennai Rally For Tamilisai Soundararajan
content: Thousands turned up to greet Amit Shah as he passed through Mylapore, the assembly constituency of BJP candidate Tamilisai Soundararajan and home to the historic Kapaleeshwarar temple

title: JD Vance To Lead US Team For Iran Talks In Pakistan: White House
content: US Vice President JD Vance will lead the US delegation for talks with Iran in Pakistan, a White House official said Sunday, after President Donald Trump indicated Vance would not make the trip.

title: "Bhai Humein Apna Jhalmuri Khilao": PM Modi Pauses For Snack Break In Bengal
content: The prime minister spent Sunday addressing four back-to-back rallies across Purulia, Jhargram, Medinipur and Bishnupur.

title: "Bengal's Language, Culture Witnessing Change Due To Infiltration": PM Modi
content: Addressing a rally in Purulia, PM Modi sought to turn the BJP's campaign in Junglemahal into a larger political battle between what he described as "development and appeasement".

content: Delhi has entered the semiconductor race, with the government drafting a policy to build capacity in chip design, research and related segments, while aiming to create jobs and attract investment.

content: US-Iran War, Ceasefire, Peace Talks Updates: The war is in its eighth week and has sent oil prices surging because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: 2 Women Killed, 31 Injured As Bus Falls Into Gorge In Rajasthan's Ajmer

content: The strike was carried out by Iranian forces and targeted a place where families linked to the Komala party were staying.

content: The RELOS pact, which will remain operational in war and peacetime, allows the two nations to save money and time in long-distance missions.

content: The suspects sent messages to the survivors through digital platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, the probe has found.
